item.title: King Charles will not live in Buckingham Palace after $487 million renovation
item.description: The king and Queen Camilla have decided instead to continue living at nearby Clarence House, a smaller, stately home.

item.title: Poland's deputy prime minister warns of possible Russian "false flag operation"
item.description: Poland's deputy prime minister tells CBS News he "wouldn't exclude the Russians doing some kind of false flag operation" to justify an attack on NATO.

item.title: Powerful earthquakes strike Venezuela, hundreds dead or injured
item.description: Venezuela's acting president said the death toll from powerful twin earthquakes was likely to rise, as USGS modeling suggested thousands may have been killed.

item.title: U.S. men suffer first 2026 World Cup loss, 3-2 to Turkey before knockout round
item.description: The U.S. men's team had already clinched its spot in the Round of 32, the knockout round, with its 2-0 win over Australia on Friday.

item.title: Iran strikes vessel in Strait of Hormuz amid debate over "transit fees"
item.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps struck a cargo ship near Oman on Thursday, further complicating the Iran-U.S. negotiations.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: See the full U.S. men's soccer schedule for the 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team kicked off its 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign against Paraguay at SoFi Stadium on Friday.

item.title: Internal emails show how RFK Jr.'s team sought to sway the CDC
item.description: A trove of emails offers a new look at how the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention navigated some of the most controversial decisions of President Trump's second term.

item.title: Venezuela quake survivors describe devastation: "Everything collapsed"
item.description: A Caracas resident told CBS News that he "started to pray" when he felt the first earthquake hit Venezuela.

item.title: Extended interview: Polish Deputy Prime Minister Radek Sikorski on the war in Ukraine
item.description: Polish Deputy Prime Minister Radek Sikorski speaks with CBS News' Aidan Stretch about the Russia's war in Ukraine, state of NATO, Ukraine-Poland relations and more.

item.title: Experts on the Venezuela earthquakes: "The scale of the devastation and damage is shocking"
item.description: Geoscientists and other scientific and humanitarian experts spoke with CBS News about the deadly earthquakes that hit Venezuela on Wednesday.

item.title: Mangione's attorneys discussed possible plea deal but talks fell apart, sources say
item.description: Mangione​ is facing both state and federal charges for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's murder in December 2024. He has pleaded not guilty in both cases.

item.title: Takeaways from Supreme Court's 2 immigration opinions
item.description: The Supreme Court issued two major immigration decisions Thursday, including one that lifts deportation restrictions for thousands of Haitian and Syrian immigrants. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson breaks them down.

item.title: Geo-technical engineering expert explains what makes the Venezuela earthquakes "such a tragedy"
item.description: Emergency crews are searching for survivors amongst the rubble after deadly back-to-back earthquakes in Venezuela. Martin Hudson, a geo-technical engineering expert, joins CBS News to discuss the rarity of the event.

item.title: Venezuelan airport outside Caracas reopens after back-to-back earthquakes
item.description: People in northern Venezuela are racing against time to pull survivors from the rubble of collapsed buildings after a pair of powerful earthquakes hit the country on Wednesday. CBS News correspondent Cristian Benavides has the latest from Bogotá, Colombia. Then, CBS News meteorologist Darren Peck joins to examine the risk of aftershocks.

item.title: WNBA suspends Alyssa Thomas for hitting Caitlin Clark's throat
item.description: No foul was initially called after Alyssa Thomas' fist made contact with Caitlin Clark's throat during a game between the Phoenix Mercury and Indiana Fever on Wednesday.

item.title: Jury remains deadlocked in trial of Palisades Fire arson suspect
item.description: A jury remains deadlocked in the trial of Jonathan Rinderknecht, the man accused of starting the blaze that turned into the deadly and destructive Palisades Fire in 2025.

item.title: Iran strikes ship in Strait of Hormuz in challenge to U.S.-Iran deal
item.description: Iran attacked a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, a U.S. official confirmed, leading a United Nations agency to pause an evacuation effort.

item.title: LA investigating construction at warehouse involved in massive fire
item.description: Los Angeles building inspectors have launched an investigation into alleged unpermitted construction at a warehouse that erupted in flames last week — its second fire in two years.

item.title: Supreme Court sides with Monsanto in case over cancer risks from weedkiller
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled that Monsanto cannot be held liable under state laws for failing to warn consumers about the alleged cancer risks of its weedkiller Roundup on its label.

item.title: Heat waves push more Europeans to embrace air conditioning
item.description: Only 20% European homes have AC, compared to 90% in the U.S., but as the climate changes, that vast gulf may be set to shrink.

item.title: Judge orders DOJ to either unredact more Epstein files or explain why it can't
item.description: A judge on Thursday ordered the Justice Department to either release unredacted versions of several files on the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein or explain why it can't do so.

item.title: Trump's obsession with SAVE America Act drives Congress into standoff
item.description: The president and his conservative allies have stymied other legislation as they unsuccessfully try to pass a voting regulations bill that lacks even simple majority support in the Senate.

item.title: Fed's preferred inflation index hit 4.1% in May amid the war with Iran
item.description: May's personal consumption expenditure index, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, rose 4.1% year to year, the highest level in three years. CBS News senior business and tech correspondent Jo Ling Kent has more.

item.title: Rep. Brad Schneider says Democrats "need to address the messaging" of democratic socialists
item.description: The establishment wing of the Democratic Party took a hit following Tuesday's primaries, in which Zohran Mamdani-backed challengers defeated Reps. Dan Goldman and Adriano Espaillat. Democratic Rep. Brad Schneider of Illinois, chair of the New Democrat Coalition, joins to assess the state of his party.

item.title: 6/25: CBS Evening News
item.description: Hundreds dead in devastating back-to-back earthquakes in Venezuela; Europe swelters under heat dome.

item.title: 6/25: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump calls for House Republicans to unify; Supreme Court hands down decisions on major immigration cases.

item.title: Taylor Swift wedding venue speculation fueled by MSG permit
item.description: There appear to be new clues about the location of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding.

item.title: Influencer faces possible execution in Dubai after allegedly fatally stabbing man
item.description: U.K. influencer Brooke George says a man she met online repeatedly assaulted her in the UAE, according to an advocacy group. Now she's facing possible execution for allegedly stabbing him to death.

item.title: Chris Evert announces her ovarian cancer has returned
item.description: American tennis legend Chris Evert announced that her ovarian cancer had returned in a social media post Thursday.
